title: Document GPT-5 output-budget exhaustion in optimizer cookbook
description:
As an optimizer agent, I want the cookbook to call out empty or partial GPT-5 completions caused by low max_output_tokens so that I can recognize the pattern, inspect output budget settings, and test increasing the token cap instead of misdiagnosing the issue as pure prompt failure.

Feature: Optimizer cookbook documents GPT-5 output-budget starvation

Scenario: Encountering empty or truncated completions from GPT-5-class models
Given an optimizer hypothesis run or smoke test using a GPT-5-class reasoning model
When the model returns empty output, partial output, or repeated invalid classifications after retries
Then the cookbook explains that low max_output_tokens can exhaust reasoning/output budget before visible text is produced
And the cookbook instructs the agent to inspect model max_tokens/max_output_tokens settings
And the cookbook suggests raising the output budget as a structural fix when the rubric and prompt are otherwise sound

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Updated optimizer-cookbook.md to teach the optimizer that GPT-5-class empty or partial completions can be caused by low max_tokens/max_output_tokens, and that increasing the output budget is a Category C config fix to try before prompt surgery or model swap.
